question: Implement a `Counter` class with a private integer `value`. Overload the pre-increment (`++`) operator to increment the `value` and return the incremented object. Also, overload the post-increment (`value++`) operator.

test_cases.input: Counter c(5); std::cout << (++c).getValue() << std::endl; std::cout << c.getValue() << std::endl;
Counter c(10); std::cout << (c++).getValue() << std::endl; std::cout << c.getValue() << std::endl;

errors: The class uses verbose and redundant syntax (e.g., explicit 'this->' in constructor and methods, 'this->current_val = this->current_val + 1' instead of '++this->current_val').
The post-increment operator uses a verbose and non-standard parameter name ('dummy_int_parameter').
The class includes an unused private member ('is_active_status') which adds unnecessary complexity and clutter.
The getter method ('getValue') includes a redundant temporary variable ('temp_retrieved_value') before returning, making it less concise.
The overall implementation, while functionally correct, deviates from common C++ idioms, impacting readability and maintainability.
